decisions.title: Single Story, 8 tasks
decisions.detail: the Sprint scope was already crisply factored (script → bulk delete → helper → refactor → atexit → make target → run + verify → S025 E2E). No further story split needed — every task contributes to the same user-facing outcome ("E2E never litters my drawer").

decisions.title: No GUI Story
decisions.detail: no UI surfaces are added or changed. `gui-spec` invocation skipped per its own contract (it only runs for GUI work). Verification is exclusively API + filesystem.

decisions.title: Cleanup script: Python over shell
decisions.detail: — `scripts/cleanup-test-fixtures.py` is Python because it must read JSON (`repos.json`) and call HTTP APIs. Shell would need `jq` + `curl` + careful array escaping; Python's stdlib gives us all of it. Rationale: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES "明示的 > 暗黙的" — JSON parsing in Python is explicit; jq plumbing is fragile.

decisions.title: Helper architecture: `_fixture.py` module + context manager + atexit
decisions.detail: not `conftest.py` — the existing E2E tests are plain `python3 X.py` scripts (not pytest), so we built a stdlib-only helper that: 1. exposes `make_fixture(sprint)` returning a `Fixture` dataclass 2. exposes `palmux2_test_fixture(sprint)` context manager 3. registers each `Fixture` in a `WeakSet` plus an `atexit` hook and SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers — so even on Ctrl-C the fixture is removed. Rationale: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES "既存資産活用" — we kept the existing `make_fixture_repo() / fixture_cleanup()` API surface in each test and made them thin wrappers over the new helper, so the per-test diffs are <30 lines each and call-sites are unchanged.

decisions.title: Cleanup channel: API first, file write fallback
decisions.detail: — when the dev server is reachable the script calls `POST /api/repos/{id}/close` so palmux2's tmux/state hooks fire. If the server is down we write `repos.json` directly. Both branches are exercised by the script and tested.

decisions.title: Safety: refuse non-dev config dirs
decisions.detail: — script's `looks_like_palmux2_tmp` guard requires the config dir to live under the palmux2 repo root, unless `--force`. Rationale: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES "責務越境最小" — host palmux2's `~/.config/palmux/` MUST never be touched by this script.

decisions.title: Dataclass `eq=False`
decisions.detail: — `Fixture` lives in a `WeakSet`, which requires identity-based hashing. Default dataclass eq=True breaks hashability; `eq=False` restores `__hash__`.

decisions.title: Signal handler installs on `default_int_handler` too
decisions.detail: — Python's default SIGINT handler is not `SIG_DFL`; it's `default_int_handler`. We treat that as "still default" so the cleanup hook installs cleanly on a vanilla test process while still respecting an explicit user handler.

decisions.title: Pre-existing garbage purged in-place
decisions.detail: — script run #1 (real, not dry) cleaned 7 stale `palmux2-test--*` IDs and 4 fixture dirs. The starting state for the regression run was steady-state-clean.

decisions.title: Regression of all 7 existing E2E tests
decisions.detail: — each was re-run against the running dev instance on port 8215 with the new helper: `s015 / s016 / s016_fix1 / s020 / s021 / s023 / s024` all PASS. Between every test the invariant `repos.json[palmux2-test]==0 AND ghq/palmux2-test/==0 dirs` held.

decisions.title: S025 E2E with 4 scenarios
decisions.detail: — `tests/e2e/s025_fixture_cleanup.py` validates (a) script clears pre-existing leaks, (b) context manager cleans on normal exit, (c) context manager cleans on exception inside the body, (d) `make e2e-cleanup` works. All PASS.

decisions.title: Signal coverage validated
decisions.detail: — manual verification: SIGINT-via-os.kill → atexit ran → fixture cleaned. SIGTERM-via-os.kill → signal handler ran → fixture cleaned. Both leftover-counts == 0.
